agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"SQL injection vulnerability in public-facing endpoint — direct string interpolation of user input into raw SQL."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized user input","risk":"Full database compromise — attacker can read, modify, or delete any data, bypass authentication, or execute admin operations via crafted customer_id or status_filter values.","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries function, query construction (lines 2-6)","reproduction":"Send request with customer_id = `' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --` or status_filter = `' UNION SELECT * FROM users --`. The f-string interpolation injects the payload directly into the SQL statement without any escaping or parameterization.","fix":"Use parameterized queries: `db_conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?\", (customer_id, status_filter))`. Never use f-strings or string concatenation for SQL query construction with user-supplied input.","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security — any public-facing endpoint must be injection-safe"},{"title":"No input validation on query parameters","risk":"Even with parameterized queries, unconstrained input allows unexpected behavior — status_filter should be validated against an allowlist of known statuses, and customer_id format should be verified.","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function signature / entry point","reproduction":"Pass status_filter = 'nonexistent_status' — returns empty results silently. Pass customer_id belonging to a different user — no authorization check, enabling IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference).","fix":"1) Validate status_filter against an enum/allowlist (e.g., `if status_filter not in VALID_STATUSES: raise ValueError`). 2) Verify the requesting user is authorized to query the given customer_id (compare against authenticated session).","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Authorization — users must only access their own delivery data"},{"title":"SELECT * exposes all columns including sensitive data","risk":"Returns every column from the deliveries table to the API caller, potentially leaking internal fields (e.g., internal_notes, cost_price, driver_ssn, auth tokens) that should never reach a public API response.","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function, SQL query","reproduction":"Call the endpoint normally — response includes all table columns regardless of what the API contract specifies.","fix":"Explicitly enumerate only the columns needed: `SELECT id, status, tracking_number, estimated_delivery FROM deliveries ...`","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Data minimization — API must only return fields defined in the response schema"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No pagination or result limit","description":"A broad query (e.g., a high-volume customer) could return unbounded rows, causing memory exhaustion or API timeouts under production load. Add LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination.","location":"search_deliveries SQL query"},{"title":"No error handling","description":"db_conn.execute can raise connection errors, timeout errors, or integrity errors. None are caught, meaning a raw database exception could propagate to the API response, leaking schema details.","location":"search_deliveries function body"}],"suggestions":["Use an ORM or query builder (e.g., SQLAlchemy) to enforce parameterization by default and reduce risk of future regressions","Add rate limiting on this public endpoint to mitigate enumeration attacks","Add logging/monitoring for anomalous query patterns (e.g., special characters in customer_id)"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code is actively exploitable the moment it is deployed to a public-facing endpoint. Any internet user can achieve full database access.","rollback_capability":"Rollback is safe but insufficient — if this code has ever been live, the database should be considered compromised and an incident response initiated.","backward_compatibility":"Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for all legitimate callers.","monitoring_coverage":"No logging or monitoring present — attacks would be invisible.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["Auth/security: no authorization check means any authenticated (or unauthenticated) user can query any customer's deliveries"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Fix parameterization, add input validation and authorization checks, then redeploy."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":99,"high_confidence_areas":["SQL injection is unambiguous — f-string interpolation of user input into SQL","IDOR risk from missing authorization check","SELECT * data exposure"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact column names in the deliveries table (assumed sensitive columns may exist)"],"assumptions":["db_conn follows Python DB-API 2.0 or similar interface supporting parameterized queries","No upstream middleware sanitizes input before this function is called"]}}
